title: hvac_failure
description: The facility's central HVAC system fails during peak summer, threatening both staff welfare and IT equipment.

timeline.title: HVAC System Offline
timeline.description: Central HVAC control panel shows a compressor fault. Cooling has stopped across all office floors.

timeline.title: Temperature Rising
timeline.description: Office temperatures rising 2°C per 15 minutes. Staff reporting discomfort. Datacenter on separate system.

timeline.title: Welfare Concern
timeline.description: Outdoor temperature is 34°C. Office areas projected to reach unsafe heat thresholds within 90 minutes.

timeline.title: HVAC Engineer On-Site
timeline.description: Facilities engineer confirms compressor failure. Replacement part has a 4-hour lead time.

timeline.title: Temporary Measures
timeline.description: Portable cooling units deployed. Staff relocated to cooler areas. Remote work activated.

timeline.title: HVAC Restored
timeline.description: Compressor replaced. Full HVAC operation restored. Office temperatures returning to normal.

node-1.title: Central HVAC Failure
node-1.situation: The central HVAC control panel has reported a compressor fault and cooling has stopped across all office floors. It is 11:00 AM on a 34°C summer day. The office areas are currently at 24°C but rising. The datacenter has a separate dedicated cooling system that is currently operating normally.
node-1.question: What is your immediate action?

options.label: A
options.text: Contact the facilities HVAC contractor immediately for an emergency callout, assess available portable cooling units, and identify which areas will become unsafe first so you can plan staff welfare actions.

options.label: B
options.text: Open all windows to cool the building naturally — the HVAC will likely restart on its own.

options.label: C
options.text: Continue normal operations and monitor — office staff can tolerate mild heat discomfort.

node-2.title: Staff Welfare Decision
node-2.situation: Office temperatures have reached 29°C and are projected to exceed 35°C within 90 minutes. The HVAC engineer has confirmed a 4-hour repair window. You have 5 portable cooling units available which can adequately cool 2 of the 4 office floors. Staff with medical conditions are reporting distress.
node-2.question: How do you manage staff welfare?

options.label: A
options.text: Deploy portable coolers to prioritise the floors with the most at-risk staff, activate remote work for staff on uncooled floors, provide water, and send any staff experiencing heat-related symptoms to a cooled area or home.

options.label: B
options.text: Ask staff to endure the heat for 4 hours until the repair is complete — operational continuity is important.

node-3.title: Datacenter Monitoring
node-3.situation: The datacenter cooling is functioning normally and is on a separate system. However, a facilities manager has suggested routing the failed HVAC ducts through the datacenter room to draw cool air from it into the offices. This would require opening the datacenter doors.
node-3.question: Do you allow the improvised cooling routing through the datacenter?

options.label: A
options.text: Reject the suggestion — opening datacenter doors disrupts airflow management, introduces humidity and contaminants, creates physical security vulnerabilities, and could overwhelm the datacenter cooling. Maintain operational separation.

options.label: B
options.text: Allow it — the datacenter cooling can handle the extra load and it will help staff welfare significantly.

node-4.title: Repair Delay
node-4.situation: The HVAC engineer has encountered a complication — the replacement compressor does not fit the original mounting brackets and a further 2-hour delay is now expected. Total downtime is now estimated at 6 hours. Several business units are demanding confirmation that operations will continue normally.
node-4.question: How do you manage the extended outage and stakeholder expectations?

options.label: A
options.text: Communicate the revised timeline honestly, expand the remote working activation to additional staff to reduce the number of people on-site, and document the welfare actions taken for both staff safety and compliance records.

options.label: B
options.text: Assure stakeholders the HVAC will be fixed within the original 4-hour estimate to avoid concern.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: The HVAC compressor has been replaced and full cooling has been restored. All staff welfare concerns were managed appropriately. No heat-related injuries occurred. A review of the HVAC maintenance schedule, portable cooling inventory, and the business continuity plan for HVAC failure has been commissioned.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
